world_name: World of Darkness - Vampire: The Masquerade
prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG gothic-punk world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: World of Darkness / Vampire: The Masquerade inspired gothic horror map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye nocturnal atlas view of one dominant gothic continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago.
Subject: one main shadowed continent with complex coastlines, misty gulfs, bays, narrow straits, broken rocky coasts, dark river deltas, inland lakes, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: gothic city islands, tiny haunted islets, craggy islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, remote atolls, ruined havens, cemetery islands, and forbidden Elysium islands. Include vampire domains, cathedral spires, sewer warrens, haunted cemeteries, fog parks, old industrial zones, werewolf-haunted forests, mountains, wetlands, tundra, and coastal cities.
Style/medium: gothic vampire masquerade city map expanded to continent scale, noir atmosphere, domain territories marked with non-text sigils, dark illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography, highly detailed.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: gothic-punk political horror, eternal night, blood moon, deep shadows, secret sea routes, ancient vampires, betrayal and debt.
Color palette: charcoal black, desaturated purple, blood red, moonlit silver, fog grey, deep blue-black ocean, muted biome colors beneath nocturnal haze.
Materials/textures: black parchment, engraved silver ink coastlines, smoky fog layers, subtle blood-red domain sigils, aged paper grain.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
